title: CDR-Based Revenue Estimation and Billing Reconciliation
dataSources: `index=cdr` with primary sourcetype `cdr:rated` (rated call-detail records post-mediation) and companion `index=billing` `sourcetype=billing:invoice` for BSS invoice or charge-summary extracts. **Index patterns**: `cdr` holds high-cardinality transactional detail (`rated_amount`, `call_duration`, `call_type`, `originating_number`, `terminating_number`) keyed by `billing_cycle` + `account_id`; `billing` holds lower-volume fiscal snapshots aligned to the same keys. **Ingest mechanisms**: (1) **DB Connect 3.x** JDBC/ODBC poll or rising-column query against mediation warehouse views (Ericsson MBM, Nokia CBIO, Openet rated tables) every 15–60 minutes for near-real-time RA, plus nightly bulk for close-the-books; (2) **batch file monitor** `[monitor:///opt/cdr/rated/*.csv]` (or ASN.1→CSV conversion drop folder) on HF/UF with `crcSalt` when carriers rewrite files in place; (3) optional **syslog** only for mediation *exceptions* (not full CDR streams). **Key auto-extracted fields** for this UC: `billing_cycle`, `account_id`, `rated_amount`, `call_duration`, `call_type`, `originating_number`, `terminating_number`, plus ingestion metadata `_indextime` for lag SLAs. **Volume**: mid-size mobile operator commonly **10–50M rated CDRs/day** (~7–35k events/sec peak after batch decompress); invoice side typically **100k–2M rows/day** depending on summarisation. **Parsing notes**: enforce `SHOULD_LINEMERGE=false` for CSV; normalise decimal separators and currency fields in `transforms.conf`; strip header duplicates; map enterprise **contract parent** IDs if invoices roll up child accounts; clock-skew adjust `_time` to **call_end** or **rating_timestamp** per 3GPP charging alignment, not file arrival time, or variance will show artificial drift.
app: Use **Splunk DB Connect 3.x** for JDBC extracts from BSS/mediation (Amdocs, CSG, Ericsson BSCS, Oracle BRM, Zuora) with Identities + Connections + Inputs defined on a heavy forwarder; schedule SQL after known ETL completion windows. Alternatively, land rated files on a mount and ingest with **batch/monitor** stanzas such as `[monitor:///opt/cdr/rated/*.csv]` with custom `props.conf` `FIELDALIAS`/`EXTRACT` for vendor-specific column order. For invoice pulls that only exist as ERP CSV exports, use the same monitor pattern against `billing` with a dedicated `TIME_FORMAT`. If mediation exposes **Kafka**/SFTP hops, prefer a HF with persistent queues. Do not funnel full rated CSV through syslog; use file or DB paths to preserve line boundaries and throughput.
spl:
index=cdr sourcetype=cdr:rated
| stats sum(rated_amount) as cdr_revenue, count as cdr_records by billing_cycle, account_id
| join type=left billing_cycle, account_id [
    search index=billing sourcetype=billing:invoice
    | stats sum(invoice_amount) as billed_revenue by billing_cycle, account_id
  ]
| eval variance_pct=round(((cdr_revenue-billed_revenue)/cdr_revenue)*100, 2)
| where abs(variance_pct) > 2
| sort -abs(variance_pct)
description: Matches aggregated rated call-detail monetary totals and record volumes per billing cycle and account against what the billing system actually invoiced, so gaps from unrated events, duplicated rows, or drops in mediation show up before month-end close.
value: Finance and revenue assurance teams recover cash that would otherwise leak through unnoticed posting errors; early variance signals shorten dispute cycles with auditors and reduce write-offs from chasing stale unreconciled balances after invoices ship.
implementation: Align `billing_cycle` and `account_id` keys exactly between mediation output and invoicing extracts; normalize currency and tax lines; whitelist known timing skew (invoice lag); tune the variance threshold after two clean cycles.
visualization: **Row 1 — KPI tiles (single value / aggregate)**: total absolute revenue at risk (`sum(abs(cdr_revenue-billed_revenue))`), count of accounts over variance threshold, worst single `variance_pct`, and median variance for quick health. **Row 2 — Trend**: timechart (`span=1d`) of mean and p95 `abs(variance_pct)` by `billing_cycle` to spot systemic mediation drift. **Row 3 — Detail table with drilldown**: columns `account_id`, `billing_cycle`, `cdr_revenue`, `billed_revenue`, `variance_pct`, `cdr_records`; row drilldown opens `index=cdr account_id=$row$` in new search. **Row 4 — Supplementary**: stacked bar of `call_type` contribution to variance for top-N accounts (detect mis-mapped service codes) plus optional geomap if `account_id` maps to region via lookup.

references.title: Splunk Lantern — Use Case Explorer: Communications
3GPP TS 32.297 — CDR File Format and Transfer
GSMA TAP3 Standard — Transferred Account Procedure
ITU-T E.425 — IP Network Performance Objectives

knownFalsePositives: **(1) Mid-cycle tariff or rate-plan changes** — rated files may reflect new price keys while invoices still use old plan codes until proration runs; mitigation: maintain a `rate_change_calendar` lookup and suppress or widen thresholds for affected `account_id` windows. **(2) Test MSISDNs and lab trunks in production feeds** — synthetic traffic creates phantom revenue; mitigation: `inputlookup` exclusion list and regex filters on `account_id`/`originating_number` ranges. **(3) CDR re-rating after disputes or regulator orders** — mediation reposts corrected rows so daily variance swings without billing movement until credit notes post; mitigation: join on rated-file version metadata if available, or alert only after **T+3** stability window. **(4) Multicurrency and tax lines** — invoices may separate tax while rated files are tax-inclusive (or vice versa); mitigation: normalise through `currency_normalization` lookup and strip tax columns before comparing. **(5) Enterprise summarised billing vs detail-rated grain** — parent account invoices aggregate child MSISDNs; naive joins look like massive gaps; mitigation: roll up CDRs to `contract_id` or use intermediary **account hierarchy** lookup. **(6) Legitimate timing skew** — invoices post T+1 after rated file cutoff; mitigation: shift search time window or compare **closed** cycles only. **(7) Duplicate batch drops** — double SFTP delivery duplicates `cdr_records` and inflates `cdr_revenue`; mediation sequence checks in Splunk (`dedup` on natural keys) or file manifest lookups reduce noise.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Rated CDR and invoice data available with aligned grain: `billing_cycle`, `account_id`, `rated_amount`, and record counts from mediation/BSS; target indexes `cdr` (`sourcetype=cdr:rated`) and `billing` (`sourcetype=billing:invoice`).
• **Splunk DB Connect 3.x** on a heavy forwarder (or supported connect node) with JDBC to mediation/BSS read replicas, **or** Universal Forwarder **batch/monitor** inputs for CSV/converted ASN.1 drops; avoid syslog for bulk CDR.
• Indexes created with retention covering **13+ billing months** for regulatory replay; license plan for **10–50M+ rated rows/day** at full operator scale (pilot at 10–20% sample if needed).
• Roles: analysts `indexes = cdr,billing`; DB Connect identity **read-only**; alert owner `schedule_search`, `list_tokens`, optional `edit_alert_actions` for ServiceNow.
• Lookups: `production_account_class` (test exclusion), `billing_cycle_calendar` (fiscal mapping), `currency_normalization`, optional `contract_hierarchy` for enterprise rollups.
• SME sign-off on **golden join keys** and documented legitimate skew (suspense, credits, proration, carry-forward).

Step 1 — Configure data collection
**Mediation / rated CDR (Path A — files):** on the UF/HF that sees the landing directory:

```ini
[monitor:///opt/cdr/rated/*.csv]
disabled = 0
index = cdr
sourcetype = cdr:rated
crcSalt = <SOURCE>
```

If vendors deliver **gzip**, use a preprocessing script or `batch://` with `move_policy` to quarantine after read. For **partial file writes**, set `ignoreOlderThan` cautiously and monitor `_indextime-_time` lag.

**props.conf (illustrative — tune to vendor):**

```ini
[cdr:rated]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
TRUNCATE = 999999
TIME_FORMAT = %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S
MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 40
REPORT-cdr_rated = cdr_rated_fields
```

**transforms.conf** (if regex-based):

```ini
[cdr_rated_fields]
DELIMS = ","
FIELDS = "billing_cycle","account_id","rated_amount","call_duration","call_type","originating_number","terminating_number"
```

**Path B — DB Connect:** create a **rising column** or **timestamp** input against mediation view `vw_rated_daily`, output `index=cdr`, `sourcetype=cdr:rated`, with SQL grouped at the same grain the alert uses (avoid double aggregation in SPL).

**Billing / invoices:** second DB Connect input (or CSV monitor) landing `index=billing`, `sourcetype=billing:invoice`, with fields `billing_cycle`, `account_id`, `invoice_amount`.

**Verification commands:**

```spl
| rest /services/data/inputs splunk_server=local | search title=*cdr* | table title, index, disabled

index=cdr sourcetype=cdr:rated earliest=-4h
| head 1
| fieldsummary

index=cdr sourcetype=cdr:rated earliest=-24h
| eval lag=_indextime-_time
| stats median(lag) p95(lag) max(lag)
```

Confirm **median lag** within business SLA (often <2h for batch, <15m for DB poll).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=cdr sourcetype=cdr:rated NOT [
    inputlookup production_account_class WHERE class="test" | fields account_id
  ]
| stats sum(rated_amount) as cdr_revenue, count as cdr_records by billing_cycle, account_id
| join type=left billing_cycle, account_id [
    search index=billing sourcetype=billing:invoice
    | stats sum(invoice_amount) as billed_revenue by billing_cycle, account_id
  ]
| fillnull value=0 billed_revenue
| eval variance_pct=if(cdr_revenue==0 OR isnull(cdr_revenue), null(),
    round(((cdr_revenue-billed_revenue)/cdr_revenue)*100, 2))
| where !isnull(variance_pct) AND abs(variance_pct) > 2
| sort - abs(variance_pct)
```

Understanding this SPL
• **Subsearch with `inputlookup`** removes known test accounts so synthetic traffic does not page RA; if lookup is empty, fall back to `where NOT match(account_id,"^(TEST|LAB)")`.
• **First `stats`** collapses rated monetary totals and **record volume** at the reconciled grain; if your BSS invoices at **contract** level, replace `account_id` with `contract_id` using `lookup contract_hierarchy`.
• **`join type=left`** preserves CDR-only rows where invoices have not yet landed, exposing **unbilled rated** usage — the highest-value RA signal.
• **`fillnull value=0 billed_revenue`** prevents null arithmetic silently nulling `variance_pct`.
• **`variance_pct` guard** skips divide-by-zero on credit-only cycles.
• **Threshold 2%** is a starting point; after two clean cycles, switch to **peer z-score** or **dynamic percentiles** via `eventstats` if static limits are too noisy.
• **Performance**: for very large joins, consider **summary indexing** or **KV store** materialisation of daily aggregates per account.

Schedule as Alert: cron `15 6 * * *` (post nightly close), time range **`-3d@d` to `@d`**, trigger **number of results > 0**, throttle **24h** on `billing_cycle,account_id`, include **top 20** in email via `fields` + `format`.

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Take **five accounts** with signed-off spreadsheet reconciliations; compare Splunk `cdr_revenue` and `billed_revenue` within **0.5%**.
(b) `index=cdr sourcetype=cdr:rated billing_cycle=<closed> | stats count sum(rated_amount)` vs mediation dashboard totals — within **1%**.
(c) Ingest a **duplicate file** in lab; confirm `cdr_records` doubles and variance exceeds threshold (proves leak detection).
(d) `| metadata type=sourcetypes index=cdr` — only expected sourcetypes; unexpected types mean route/parsing drift.
(e) Multicurrency: apply FX lookup and re-run; variances should **shrink**, not grow.
(f) Impersonate `ra_analyst` role — search must not return **index missing** errors.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: **Row 1** KPI tiles (revenue at risk, account count, worst variance, rolling 7d median). **Row 2** timechart of `mean(abs(variance_pct))` by `billing_cycle`. **Row 3** sortable table with drilldown to raw CDR. **Row 4** supplementary `call_type` stack for top-variance accounts.
Alerting: **ServiceNow** incident with `billing_cycle`, `account_id`, amount fields; **Teams** optional for P1 cycles; attach **deep link** to saved search.
Runbook: (1) Check ITSM for active **rate deployment**. (2) Verify mediation file sequence / DB watermark. (3) Compare invoice extract timestamp. (4) Open vendor ticket if duplicate/drop. (5) Finance journal if true under/over-bill. (6) Record in RA register. (7) Tune threshold or lookup if false positive.

Extended mediation and BSS correlation notes

Mediation pipelines that produce `cdr:rated` typically concatenate multiple upstream record types (voice MOC/MTC, SMS, MMS, GPRS/5G PDU sessions). When your UC only validates monetary totals without checking record-type mix, a compensating error (more voice offset by less data) may still net to a low variance_pct while traffic is materially wrong. Add a QA sibling search—not part of this alert—which breaks out `stats sum(rated_amount) by call_type` per `billing_cycle`, and reconcile any sudden shifts against product launches or interconnect price changes documented in telecom product management calendars.

Ericsson Mobility Billing Mediation (MBM), Nokia Convergent Billing Intelligent Operations (CBIO lineage), Openet Falcon, MATRIXX, or in-house Hadoop rating jobs all stamp subtly different timestamps: `exchange_record_start`, `billing_period_start`, file cut-off UTC. Normalise `_time` to the mediation **rating completion** clock that finance uses when they say “rated as of”; otherwise nightly batch boundaries will systematically mis-join invoices that book on fiscal calendar midnight in local time versus UTC anchors.

ASN.1 or BER-encoded CDRs are rarely ingested verbatim into Splunk. Partners usually convert via off-box utilities into UTF-8 CSV with explicit headers. Maintain those conversion utilities under change control—the most common Splunk ingestion failure mode is header drift when a mediation minor release adds trailing nullable columns without communication. Splunk silently maps fields wrongly until fieldsummary shows anomalies (e.g., `rated_amount` contains MSISDNs). Automated schema tests using `delta` comparisons of fieldsummary week-over-week close this gap.

Billing systems such as Amdocs Optima, CSG Interstate, Ericsson BRM/BSCS, or Oracle Billing and Revenue Management may emit **summarised invoices** nightly while rated files contain **subscriber-level grain**. Solve this hierarchical mismatch with a deterministic roll-up SPL pass or enrichment lookup `account_id_parent` stamped at index-time via KV collection refresh from BSS API. Regulatory environments sometimes forbid storing raw MSISDN in Splunk; hash or tokenise before join keys are published to shared indexes.

Operational risk registers should record each material false positive dismissal with root-cause taxonomy (timing, tariff, FX, hierarchy, duplication). After six months, those labels feed supervised ML on features derived from Splunk aggregates—optional future wave—without changing today's deterministic alert.

Throughput engineering: joins at rated+invoice grain for millions of accounts may exceed subsearch defaults. Tune `limits.conf` `[subsearch] maxout` responsibly, favour **summary indexing** emitting `billing_cycle`, `account_id`, `cdr_revenue`, `billed_revenue` hourly, then alert against summaries. Licensing impact drops materially when raw CDR is retained in cold buckets but alerting runs on warmed summaries aligned to RA SLA.

Governance checkpoints: RACI separating mediation platform owners vs billing application owners prevents alert tuning arguments in incident bridges. SLA for investigation start should mirror TM Forum RA/MM guidance — initial triage <4 business hours for high-severity deltas affecting top-N revenue-at-risk populations.
Executive reconciliation narrative and forensic drill paths

Executive stakeholders rarely consume raw SPL; translate alert rows into CFO-friendly bullet narratives using scheduled summary index lookups that annotate each account variance with directional risk (recognition delay versus permanent leakage). Persist enumerated story labels such as suspected_dup_file, tariff_proration_overlap, multicurrency_residual, unsubmitted_invoice_batch, orphaned_rated_segments.

Forensic drills should chain from high variance account to granular evidence without exporting entire CDR warehouses to desktops. Pivot from SPL table rows using tokenised drills scoped to billing_cycle and account_id across cdr:rated and optional billing detail sourcetypes—even when aggregates summarise, ephemeral detail extracts accelerate dispute letters within statutory windows.

Tape rotation and archival: ensure Splunk archival policy aligns with finance document retention so provenance timestamps show when mediation files were searchable relative to ledger posting—a frequent external audit pivot.

Synthetic monitoring: inject sentinel accounts with predictable tiny balances weekly; variance should read near zero plus noise. Positive drift triggers engineering tickets before widespread RA breakage.

Operational analytics on fatigue: Splunk audit searches counting suppressions guide staffing models; escalate retuning backlog when suppression ratios exceed governance targets.

Edge roaming inclusion: if domestic and roaming rated files split across indexes or sourcetypes, union them before aggregates or holiday peaks distort corporate totals.

Technical debt vigilance: when mediation migrates to cloud object storage, POSIX mount ingest paths drift—staging validation parity prevents silent gaps until month-end.
Quarter-close acceleration and deterministic replay controls

During fiscal quarter close, treasury and RA teams need deterministic replay of mediation extracts as they existed at cutoff timestamps—not merely latest Splunk data—because auditors compare point-in-time balances. Maintain snapshot labels indexed as fields (`rated_snapshot_id`) or a sourcetype `cdr:rated_snapshot` emitted when finance formally locks a billing run. Alerts for closed cycles query snapshot indexes only; open cycles remain on the mutable ingestion stream.

Queue-depth indicators from mediation middleware (Kafka lag, stale SFTP files) correlate with unexplained downward variance spikes—join modest-volume telemetry feeds (`sourcetype=mediation:health`) keyed by ingestion route to distinguish infrastructure backlog from monetary leakage.

Operationalise segregation-of-duties: DB Connect secret rotation approvals flow through ITSM; Splunk platform admins without finance roles cannot escalate DB identities that read invoice GL balances containing sensitive recognition data.

Suspected duplicate file isolation uses staging-index ingestion capturing file hash metadata (`file_sha`) via scripted prelude. Incident war rooms compare staging vs production cardinality before admitting new files into the authoritative mediation path.

Pre-holiday load tests replay join fan-out at twice expected cardinalities on anonymised or synthetic datasets to validate search-head auto-scaling and macro-expanded limits behave within SLA.

Finally, version-control threshold-policy documentation externally and cite change IDs inside saved search descriptions so tuning history is audit-friendly.
Appendix — join performance, data quality rules, and Splunk admin knobs

For join-heavy workloads exceeding SH memory, materialise hourly or daily summaries with `collect` into `summary_ra_cdr_billing` capturing pre-joined `billing_cycle`, `account_id`, `cdr_revenue`, `billed_revenue`, `cdr_records` — then alert runs O(accounts) not O(events). Use `enablePreview=false` in jobs and `requireSuccess=true` in alert actions to avoid partial results during indexer rolling restarts.

Implement data quality rules as **pre-alert** saved searches: flag null `rated_amount` spikes, sudden sourcetype drift, or abrupt `host` silence from DB Connect execute nodes — these infrastructure signals should page platform teams before RA misinterprets ingestion gaps as revenue loss.

Document `limits.conf` tuning only with CAB approval: raising `subsearch` `maxout` masks underlying grain mismatches—you usually need better keys, not larger subsearches.

Operational handover: include sample **onboarding lab** walkthrough reproducing a benign 1% variance explaining proration so new analysts internalise expected behaviour before touching production macros.
Final controls — audit exports, RBAC reviews, and deterministic macros

Bundle savedsearch results weekly into `ra_evidence` summary index with immutable `_time` ingestion from a dedicated service account restricted to `input` capability on that index only—evidence packs for auditors then query a narrow index rather than entire `cdr` history.

Quarterly RBAC review verifies no single human holds both `splunk_admin` and `db_connect_admin` along with `ra_analyst` interpreting money—segregate using groups and service identities.

Wrap production SPL in **macros** (`macro_ra_cdr_variance`) parameterising threshold and index names—prevents ad-hoc divergence between dashboard and alert SPL copies.
Indexing note: mark `rated_amount`, `billing_cycle`, and `account_id` as **indexed fields** only after confirming cardinality and disk trade-offs—accelerated filtering on giants like `account_id` can explode index size; often `tstats`/summary paths outperform.
Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Spike only on enterprise accounts** — summarised invoice grain; fix join keys / hierarchy lookup.
• **Null `rated_amount` everywhere** — field alias wrong; `| head 1` and compare `_raw` to `props`.
• **Zero billing rows after join** — `billing_cycle` format mismatch; normalise with `replace()` or `billing_cycle_calendar` lookup.
• **Noise after marketing double-data promos** — promotional rated rows without invoice lines; temporary lookup suppress.
• **Sudden halving of counts** — partial SFTP; check transfer logs and mediation alarms.
• **Variance flips sign daily** — re-rating in flight; delay alert to T+2 post file stable.
• **Search slow or times out** — reduce window, pre-aggregate with summary index, or push stats to DBX output tables hourly.
grandmaExplanation: We line up what our phone network says we should charge with what the bills actually show. When the two stories disagree by more than a little, we catch money walking out the door before the books close.
telcoUseCase: Revenue assurance — reconcile rated CDR totals against billing invoices to detect mediation drops, double-rated calls, and unbilled usage that erode margin.
